decks.notes: The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly deploy low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while maintaining a tempo advantage with cheap spells like Fiery Impulse and Academic Dispute. This deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game and finish them off with powerful prowess creatures like Stormchaser's Talent. By using efficient removal spells like Reckless Rage and Stock Up, the deck can control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot. The inclusion of Vivi Ornitier and Experimental Synthesizer provides additional card advantage and options for generating value.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to build a fast and aggressive board presence using efficient creatures like Archon of Emeria, Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, Skyclave Apparition, and Elvish Mystic. The deck also includes cards like Collected Company and Michelangelo's Technique for additional creature generation and control. The inclusion of Enduring Innocence and Anointed Peacekeeper helps to disrupt opponent strategies, while Ouroboroid provides resilience. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents early in the game with a combination of powerful creatures and disruptive elements.

This deck aims to aggressively pressure the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures like Bloodtithe Harvester and Graveyard Trespasser, powerful removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrade, and disruptive hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress. The deck also utilizes powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Sokenzan, Crucible of Defiance to close out games quickly. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive game plan, with a mix of fast lands and shock lands. Overall, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with a fast, aggressive game plan.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, then buffing them with spells like Fiery Impulse and Stormchaser's Talent to deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes bounce effects like Boomerang Basics and Den of the Bugbear to disrupt the opponent's board state. With aggressive creatures and efficient spells, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game and secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing various disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Duress, backed up by removal like Fatal Push. The main win condition is to outvalue the opponent through card advantage and tempo plays with cards like Yorion, Sky Nomad and Stormchaser's Talent. The deck also aims to stall the game with defensive cards like Fear of Isolation and Nowhere to Run, while generating additional resources with cards like Cryogen Relic and Grim Bauble. It also includes a mix of lands to ensure consistent mana base and card draw options.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost spells and creatures to deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. It utilizes cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Stormchaser's Talent to continuously put pressure on the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of card draw spells like Stock Up and Consider ensures that the deck can maintain its aggression throughout the game.

The main concept of this deck is to use efficient low-cost creatures and spells to apply pressure on the opponent quickly. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to deal damage to the opponent as quickly as possible. The deck also includes cards like Academic Dispute and Experimental Synthesizer to generate card advantage and maintain tempo. Overall, the deck focuses on aggressive early game plays and maintaining pressure throughout the game.

This UW Control deck focuses on card advantage, removal, and stalling the game until it can establish a dominant position. The deck uses cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, Supreme Verdict, and Dovin's Veto to control the board and prevent the opponent from mounting a strong offense. It also includes draw spells like Memory Deluge and Consult the Star Charts to ensure consistency and give the player access to the right answers at the right time. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and win through incremental advantage and efficient control.

This red aggro deck focuses on fast, aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. With a low mana curve and efficient spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game. Cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage benefit from casting multiple spells in a turn, while Den of the Bugbear provides additional creature pressure. With a mix of cheap creatures and direct damage spells, this deck looks to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost, aggressive creatures like Emberheart Challenger and Monastery Swiftspear, while using burn spells like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage to control the board. The deck also utilizes card draw and mana efficiency to maintain pressure and fill the board with threats. By combining creature aggression with burn spells and card advantage, this deck aims to win the game swiftly through tempo and aggression, putting constant pressure on the opponent from the early game.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger to apply constant pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to remove threats and clear the way for attacks. With cards like Heroes' Hangout and Academic Dispute providing card advantage, the deck aims to maintain a fast pace and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creatures and burn spells. The mana base is also carefully constructed with lands like Spirebluff Canal and Steam Vents to ensure consistent access to the deck's colors.

This Rakdos Aggro deck focuses on aggressive plays using low-cost creatures and disruption spells to put pressure on the opponent quickly. The deck also includes cards that provide additional value by enabling graveyard interactions and card advantage. By combining aggression with disruption and value generation, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a fast victory.

This deck focuses on using the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It includes various cards to protect and support this strategy, such as Iron-Shield Elf, Sheltered by Ghosts, and Fatal Push. The deck also includes cards like Thoughtseize for disruption and Monument to Endurance for additional synergy with the combo pieces. Overall, the goal of the deck is to quickly assemble the combo and use it to dominate the game.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Lotus Field with various cards such as Arboreal Grazer, Vizier of Tumbling Sands, and Hidden Strings to generate a large amount of mana quickly. This allows the player to cast powerful spells like Omniscience, Ultimatum','','');">Emergent Ultimatum, and Peer into the Abyss to overwhelm their opponent and gain a significant advantage. The deck includes additional cards for control and recursion to ensure the combo goes off smoothly and consistently.

The main concept of this deck is to assemble the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate a powerful synergistic board state. By utilizing various control elements like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize, as well as protection from cards like Sheltered by Ghosts and Guardian of New Benalia, the deck aims to ensure the combo pieces are able to come together smoothly. The deck also includes a mix of lands to support the mana requirements of the combo while also providing some flexibility in terms of resource management.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that help control the opponent's hand and board state, as well as mana fixing and removal spells to protect the combo pieces. The strategy revolves around setting up the combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan, leading to a decisive victory.
